reasoning: The resolution is strictly based on the "Vaccine(s)" column for "Gonorrhea" on the Wikipedia page "List of infectious diseases". This list is notoriously poorly maintained; the "Vaccine(s)" column is currently completely blank for almost every disease, including COVID-19, Influenza, and Hepatitis B. Although the UK launched a gonorrhea vaccination program in August 2025 using the meningitis vaccine Bexsero (4CMenB), the Wikipedia table was not updated by the knowledge cutoff (late October 2025). Given that the whole column is systematically neglected, it is highly improbable that an editor will randomly populate the Gonorrhea cell before January 2026. Furthermore, no brand-new, explicit gonorrhea vaccines are slated for phase 3 completion and approval in this tight window. I give it a baseline 5-6% chance purely to account for random editor behavior or forecast-induced Wikipedia edits.
